I wanted to do a quick update on this fish tank. I have the 5 gallon tank. As I started to purchase plants and decorative items for the tank I was purchasing them based on the dimensions that are given on here to my surprise the tank is not as large as they say that it is and it does not hold 5 gallons of water even without rock in there. The actual dimensions of the tank portion without the lid and without vase are 10 inches high X 13 1/2 inches wide X 6.5” sides without bow, going to 10” at the very front of the bow. Another thing that I found very helpful was reading through all of the questions and comments and finding one that told me how to quiet this filter down. They do not tell you that you have to fill the water all the way to the base of the filter which completely submerses it. Even then it does still make a slight grinding noise but much better having added the extra half inch of water to it that I was lacking. I do still wish that it had been the correct size that they say which is 15 x 15 1/2 x 10 essentially I would love to have been able to put a few things or a little bit taller in the tank. Just so that you know the highest that you really should put in there is about 9 to 9 1/2 inches anything else than that will be above the water line. And one last thing for me anyways the where the water flows onto this flat filter only about half of the filter actually gets used, And my tank is level. Overall I guess they just really need a better filter. I thought this tank because I like the way that it looks, however I am wishing now that I had bought one of the panoramic, tall round, or the 6 1/2 gallon tank or even go out to a 10 gallon tank, so that I could’ve had a few more fish because I’m not one to just have a single Beita in a 5 gallon tank or in this case it’s actually about 4 gallons even without rock.

Our dog is a medium sized Shephard mix and is hard on dog toys. First thing she goes for is the squeaker, then the stuffing. This large log had six squirrels in it so we figured it might last longer with her than a few days.

We had to show her there were squirrels in it after she tried chewing the stump. So she goes for the squirrels and shook the stump like a dog will when playing. One squirrel jettisoned from a hole and flew 15 feet over the Christmas tree. Another one went the opposite direction 10-feet and hit my 20-year-old grandson in the face. We couldn't stop laughing.

Now we plug the top with some of her older toys so she has to dig them out of the holes. A couple of squirrels have already been destuffed and de-squeaked, but she loves this toy. How long will it last and will we buy more squirrels? Don't know. But as long as the stump holds out we can stick any matter of toys inside it.

UPDATE; Day 3 and three squirrels totally dead. Some cardboard insert gone from stump.

Unfortunately, this pad is far flimsier than other self-warming pads and only so-so plush. The pad is much thinner than others we've purchased. That reflective material inside barely crinkles. That might be good for cats who are scared of the noise. They grey material on top is very slick and does NOT radiate the warmth as well as other self-warming pads.

Also, there was NO product description or label describing the pad -- just a clear plastic bag. If one were to send this to someone else, it would just appear to be a cat pad: no reference to it being self-warming.

In short, it's okay but we'll be looking to buy a better quality self-warming pad in the future.

I don't know specifically what Purina "TidyCats" has done with their Pure Nature litter, but they surely have changed the formula and for the WORSE.

I've used this for at least 3 years. Loved the odor blocking for feces and how nicely it clumped for urine for the living room litter tray (use another brand, unscented, for other trays).

That was then; seems that around 4+ months ago the formula has changed. There is absolutely NO clumping whatsoever and the urine drains straight to the bottom of the tray where it congeals into a wet mess. I initially thought my cat was scraping to the bottom, but no .... watched her wee on the top and not cover it. Nice (not !!)to find her wee had drained straight to the bottom. As others noted, there is more tracking which was NOT the case until recently.

I gave this product 3 stars because it has absolutely made everyone I've shown it to laugh hysterically.  You should have seen my Veterinarian holding my dog Murphy in one arm and showing his Wisdom Panel 2.0 Breed Identification DNA Test results to everyone in her office.  They all got a great laugh and you just can't buy that kind of laughter.  We certainly made their day with Murphy's results.  If I'm to believe Wisdom Panel my dog is a Chinese Shar-Pei, Pekingese, Whippet Mix.  The vet still has him listed as a Schnauzer/Terrier Mix in his file and no matter how much I beg and plead with her to list him as a Chinese Shar-Pei, Pekingese, Whippet Mix She just won't do it!  I don't like to throw around words like Bamboozled but in this case I think it might apply.  I even waited until my little Murphy reached maturity before posting this (along with his picture) to make sure that he didn't change into a Shar-Pei, Pekingese, Whippet Mix as he matured but alas, he's still a Schnauzer/Terrier Mix in the eyes of the law (that's what his county licensing records reflect because my vet is a meanie)!!

I purchased these to replace the regular cap looking can lids that we kept losing in drawers around the house. I liked the paw print design because it easily marks the item as for doggy only, so the lids wouldn't be reused for people food.

These definitely work as designed. There are two grooves around the edge so that you can use three different can sizes with these lids. This is helpful if you use different types of dog food and the can sizes vary. We don't, so this feature is a little annoying because it makes it *slightly* harder to put the lid on. You have to align the grooves to snap the lid on and it takes just a bit more focus than popping a regular can cover on. This is a little nit and doesn't really take away from the product.

My other very small nit is the paw print shape. I purchased these for the paw print shape and wish I had purchased a regular circular shape because the paw takes up more room in an already crammed fridge. It's only slightly bigger, but every inch counts! Again, this is a very small complaint.

Our set came with a dog food spoon and we love the spoon! It's really long, so you can dig the food out of the can and has the heart shape to make scraping and mixing dry food and wet food easier. This spoon was something we didn't know we needed and now can't live without.

Overall, I think this is a good product and gets the job done. It's especially great if you use multiple can sizes.

The latch always feels a little flimsy and weird. You have to pinch both sides and pull them up while lifting to open the gate. It's not easy. I fumble with the stupid thing all the time - I'm hoping that means that the little one won't figure it out either. All in all, this gate has worked well for us. One quirk (besides the hard latch) is that when you close the gate you have to push down to lock it into place, else you can just lift the gate and it will open again without having to use the special lever mechanism. Not a deal breaker, but definitely something to remember.
